headline: UR-ENERGY FIRES UP SECOND U.S. URANIUM MINE AT WYOMING'S SHIRLEY BASIN
snippet: Ur-Energy (TSX/NYSE: URG) has commenced uranium solution recovery at its Shirley Basin ISR project in Wyoming — its second operating U.S. mine — expanding total licensed production and toll-processing capacity to 4.2 million lbs U₃O₈ per year. Shirley Basin holds 9.1 million lbs in M&I resources across an anticipated nine-year mine life. First loaded resin is expected at the Lost Creek processing facility this summer pending final regulatory sign-off.

headline: CONTANGO SILVER & GOLD OUTLINES $63M EXPLORATION BLITZ ACROSS ALASKA AND BC
snippet: Newly merged Contango Silver & Gold — formed from the combination of Contango Ore and Dolly Varden Silver — is deploying $63 million on exploration in 2026. The headline commitment is a $25 million, 40,000-metre infill drill program at Kitsault Valley in Northern BC, one of Canada's most advanced critical metals projects, with additional campaigns across its Alaska gold portfolio.

headline: CRITICAL METALS GAINS 92.5% CONTROL OF GREENLAND'S TANBREEZ HEAVY RARE EARTH DEPOSIT
snippet: Greenland approved Critical Metals Corp.'s transfer of the final 50.5% stake in Tanbreez Mining Greenland A/S, lifting total ownership to 92.5%. Tanbreez is considered one of the world's largest undeveloped heavy rare earth deposits outside China, hosting all eight HREEs needed for defence and clean energy applications. The company holds a US$120M EXIM Bank letter of intent, and a 150-tonne bulk sample program is planned for June 2026. Shares surged more than 23% on the news.

headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,700 AS US-IRAN CEASEFIRE HOPES FADE AND HORMUZ CLOSURE WEIGHS
snippet: Gold recovered to $4,723/oz on April 23 after dipping on brief US-Iran peace talk optimism, then rising again to $4,736 on April 24. The metal is up 42% year-over-year and 4.3% over the past month. The VanEck GDX gold miners ETF has returned 108% over the past year as mining equities finally close their valuation gap with the underlying metal. JPMorgan and Goldman see gold in a $4,000–$6,300 range for 2026.

headline: FITZROY MINERALS INKS PUCOBRE JOINT DEVELOPMENT DEAL FOR CHILE COPPER PROJECT
snippet: Fitzroy Minerals (TSX-V) signed a non-binding LOI with Chilean producer Sociedad Punta del Cobre S.A. (Pucobre) for the Buen Retiro oxide copper project in Chile's Punta del Cobre belt. Key terms: Pucobre takes a 30% claw-back right, reimburses US$10.2M in eligible technical expenses, and commits 800 tonnes per month of electrowon copper processing capacity at its existing Planta Biocobre EW facility. The deal de-risks the oxide heap-leach development path while Fitzroy advances sulphide exploration.

headline: CHINA'S SULFURIC ACID EXPORT BAN FROM MAY 1 THREATENS 20% OF GLOBAL COPPER SUPPLY
snippet: Beijing will ban sulphuric acid exports starting May 1 as the Strait of Hormuz closure cuts global seaborne sulphur supply by roughly 50%. About 20% of world copper output relies on acid-leach SX-EW processes, with Chilean and Congolese oxide operations most exposed. Analysts estimate costs at affected mines could rise 40–60% as buyers scramble for spot cargoes. The ban also disrupts fertiliser supply chains, adding to food-price inflation risk.

headline: WORLD SILVER SURVEY 2026: SIXTH STRAIGHT SUPPLY DEFICIT AT 46.3 MILLION OUNCES
snippet: The World Silver Survey 2026 projects a 46.3 million ounce supply deficit this year — up 15% from 40.3 million ounces in 2025 — making it the sixth consecutive year where demand outstrips total supply. Since 2021, 762 million troy ounces have been drawn from above-ground stocks, a drawdown the survey describes as having 'no modern precedent.' Investment demand gains are offsetting softness in industrial and jewellery consumption.

headline: FED DELAYS RATE CUTS TO LATE 2026 AS HORMUZ WAR KEEPS INFLATION ELEVATED
snippet: A Reuters poll of economists shows the Fed's first rate cut has been pushed to Q4 2026 as the Strait of Hormuz closure sustains energy price inflation and complicates the central bank's easing timeline. Delayed rate relief — combined with persistent geopolitical risk — is widely cited as a structural tailwind for gold and silver. The Hormuz crisis has been underway since late February when US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran began.

headline: GOLD MINERS STILL UNDERVALUED AT $4,700 GOLD — STRUCTURAL BULL MARKET INTACT
snippet: Despite a 65% gold rally in 2025 and GDX up 108% year-over-year, senior gold producers still trade at just 9.6× forward cash flow — barely above the five-year average of 8.35×. Haywood forecasts a $4,906/oz average gold price for 2026. BMO's top picks for further leverage include Aris Mining, Discovery Silver, Montage Gold, and Newmont. Central bank buying remains the dominant structural driver, with emerging-market purchases projected at 850 tonnes for 2026.

headline: GOLD-SILVER RATIO FALLS FROM 105 PEAK — A HISTORICALLY BULLISH SIGNAL FOR SILVER
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April 2026 and is now contracting — a pattern that has historically preceded sharp silver outperformance. Bank of America has set a 2026 silver price target of $135–$309 per ounce. Chinese silver imports exceeded 790 tonnes in early 2026, driven partly by solar manufacturing demand, while the silver market heads into a sixth consecutive structural deficit. Silver has already climbed more than 150% over the past year.
